rice.irrigationMethod: Flood irrigation with alternate wetting and drying (AWD)
rice.irrigationSchedule: Maintain 5cm standing water during tillering and flowering. Drain 10 days before harvest.

fertilizer.organic: 5-10 tonnes FYM per hectare before transplanting

pests.name: Stem borer
pests.prevention: Use pheromone traps, apply Cartap hydrochloride

pests.name: Brown plant hopper
pests.prevention: Avoid excess nitrogen, use neem-based sprays

rice.harvestIndicators: Grain moisture 20-22%, 80% panicles turned golden, straw is yellow-green
rice.weatherSensitivity: Sensitive to cold during flowering (<15C causes sterility). Heavy rain at harvest causes grain discoloration.

wheat.irrigationSchedule: Crown root initiation (21 days), tillering (40 days), jointing (60 days), flowering (80 days), milking (100 days), dough (115 days)

fertilizer.organic: 5 tonnes FYM per hectare before sowing

wheat.harvestIndicators: Golden yellow color, grain hard and cannot be dented by thumbnail, moisture 12-14%
wheat.weatherSensitivity: Terminal heat stress (>35C in March-April) reduces grain filling. Untimely rain at maturity causes grain shriveling.

maize.irrigationSchedule: Critical stages: knee-high (30 days), tasseling (55 days), silking (65 days), grain filling (80 days). Avoid waterlogging.

pests.name: Fall armyworm
pests.prevention: Use pheromone traps, spray Emamectin benzoate

pests.name: Stem borer
pests.prevention: Release Trichogramma cards, apply Carbofuran granules

maize.harvestIndicators: Husk turns brown, kernels are hard and glossy, black layer visible at kernel base, moisture 20-25%
maize.weatherSensitivity: Drought stress at silking causes poor kernel set. Waterlogging at any stage causes root rot.

soybean.irrigationMethod: Rainfed preferred; supplemental sprinkler if dry spell > 15 days
soybean.irrigationSchedule: Critical moisture needed at flowering (35-40 days) and pod filling (65-75 days). Avoid waterlogging.

pests.name: Girdle beetle
pests.prevention: Early sowing, spray Triazophos at 30 days

pests.name: Yellow mosaic virus
pests.prevention: Use resistant varieties (JS 335, JS 9560), control whitefly vector

soybean.harvestIndicators: Leaves turn yellow and drop, pods turn brown, seeds rattle in pods, moisture 13-14%
soybean.weatherSensitivity: Excess rain during pod maturity causes seed deterioration. Drought during flowering reduces pod count by 40-60%.

mustard.irrigationMethod: Flood or sprinkler; drought tolerant crop
mustard.irrigationSchedule: First irrigation at 25-30 days (pre-flowering), second at 55-60 days (pod formation). Third only if very dry.

fertilizer.organic: 4-5 tonnes FYM, add Sulphur 20 kg/ha (critical for oil content)

pests.name: Aphids
pests.prevention: Spray Dimethoate 30EC when population builds on terminals

pests.name: Painted bug
pests.prevention: Dust Malathion 5% in early morning when bugs are sluggish

pests.name: White rust / Alternaria
pests.prevention: Spray Mancozeb 75WP at first sign of spots

mustard.harvestIndicators: Pods turn yellow-brown, seeds turn brown-black, moisture 10-12%, siliquae start shattering
mustard.weatherSensitivity: Frost at flowering stage causes severe damage. Fog increases Alternaria risk. Early heat (>30C in Feb) forces early maturity.

millet.irrigationMethod: Rainfed; supplemental irrigation only during prolonged dry spell
millet.irrigationSchedule: If irrigated: at tillering (20 days), flowering (40 days), grain filling (55 days). Highly drought tolerant.

fertilizer.organic: 4-5 tonnes FYM or compost per hectare

pests.name: Shoot fly
pests.prevention: Early sowing, seed treatment with Imidacloprid

pests.name: Downy mildew
pests.prevention: Seed treatment with Metalaxyl, use resistant hybrids

millet.harvestIndicators: Ear heads turn grey-brown, grain hardens, thumbnail test positive, moisture 12-14%
millet.weatherSensitivity: Tolerates heat and drought well. Susceptible to waterlogging. Cool temperatures at flowering reduce seed set.

chickpea.irrigationMethod: Sprinkler or flood; sensitive to excess moisture
chickpea.irrigationSchedule: Pre-sowing irrigation essential. One irrigation at flowering (45-50 days) if no rain. Avoid irrigation at pod maturity.

fertilizer.organic: Rhizobium seed inoculation + 2-3 tonnes FYM. Being a legume, it fixes its own nitrogen.

pests.name: Pod borer (Helicoverpa)
pests.prevention: Spray HaNPV or Bt at 50% flowering, use pheromone traps

pests.name: Wilt (Fusarium)
pests.prevention: Use resistant varieties (JG 11, JG 14), seed treatment with Trichoderma

pests.name: Dry root rot
pests.prevention: Avoid moisture stress, treat seed with Carbendazim

chickpea.harvestIndicators: Plants turn yellow, leaves drop, pods turn brown and dry, seeds rattle, moisture 10-12%
chickpea.weatherSensitivity: Excess rain or humidity causes Ascochyta blight. Frost at flowering causes pod abortion. Needs cool dry weather for pod filling.

cotton.irrigationMethod: Drip irrigation recommended; furrow irrigation conventional
cotton.irrigationSchedule: Critical: squaring (45 days), flowering (60-90 days), boll development (90-120 days). Skip irrigation during boll opening.

pests.name: Bollworm
pests.prevention: Bt cotton + refuge planting, spray Emamectin at economic threshold

pests.name: Pink bollworm
pests.prevention: Timely sowing, pheromone traps, destroy crop residues

cotton.harvestIndicators: Bolls burst open fully, cotton is fluffy white, pick in 3-4 rounds, moisture 8-10%
cotton.weatherSensitivity: Excess rain during boll opening causes staining. High humidity increases pest pressure. Needs warm days and cool nights for fiber quality.

lentil.irrigationMethod: Light sprinkler or rainfed in most areas
lentil.irrigationSchedule: Pre-sowing irrigation, one at branching (35-40 days) if dry. Excess moisture causes root rot.

pests.name: Rust
pests.prevention: Spray Mancozeb at first symptoms, use resistant varieties

pests.name: Aphids
pests.prevention: Spray Dimethoate when colonies appear on terminals

lentil.harvestIndicators: Plants turn golden-brown, pods dry out, seeds harden, moisture 10-12%
lentil.weatherSensitivity: Excess moisture causes rust and root rot. Needs cool, dry post-flowering weather. Frost at flowering damages pods.

jute.irrigationMethod: Mostly rainfed (monsoon crop); field should not waterlog
jute.irrigationSchedule: Depends on monsoon. If dry spell > 10 days in July-August, provide one irrigation.

pests.name: Stem rot
pests.prevention: Avoid waterlogging, treat seed with Carbendazim

jute.harvestIndicators: Harvest at small pod stage (120-140 days), stem starts browning at base, flowers are falling
jute.weatherSensitivity: Needs consistent monsoon moisture. Drought causes fiber quality decline. Excess waterlogging causes root rot.

mungbean.irrigationSchedule: One irrigation at flowering if no rain. Avoid excess moisture.
mungbean.harvestIndicators: Pods turn black/brown, pick in 2-3 rounds as pods mature unevenly
mungbean.weatherSensitivity: Excess rain at maturity causes sprouting in pods. Needs warm humid weather for vegetative growth.

blackgram.irrigationSchedule: Similar to mungbean - one irrigation at flowering if needed
blackgram.harvestIndicators: Pods turn black, harvest when 80% pods are mature
blackgram.weatherSensitivity: Excess moisture at harvest causes seed discoloration. Drought at flowering reduces yield.

pigeonpeas.irrigationSchedule: One irrigation at flowering, one at pod filling if dry

pests.name: Wilt (Fusarium)
pests.prevention: Resistant varieties, seed treatment with Trichoderma

pigeonpeas.harvestIndicators: Pods turn brown and dry, seeds harden, moisture 10-12%
pigeonpeas.weatherSensitivity: Waterlogging at any stage is fatal. Needs well-drained soil. Frost damages flowers.

mothbeans.irrigationSchedule: Completely rainfed in most areas. One protective irrigation only in extreme drought.
mothbeans.harvestIndicators: Pods turn brown, plant dries out, harvest at full maturity
mothbeans.weatherSensitivity: Extremely drought tolerant. Cannot tolerate waterlogging. Best suited for arid regions.

kidneybeans.irrigationMethod: Sprinkler or furrow, grown in hill areas with natural moisture
kidneybeans.irrigationSchedule: Flowering and pod filling are critical. 3-4 irrigations if no rain.

pests.name: Root rot
pests.prevention: Avoid waterlogging, treat seed with Thiram

kidneybeans.harvestIndicators: Pods turn yellow and dry, seeds rattle inside, moisture 12-14%
kidneybeans.weatherSensitivity: Prefers cool climates (15-25C). Cannot tolerate frost or extreme heat. Grown mainly in hills.

banana.irrigationSchedule: Daily drip: 10-15 liters/plant/day in summer, 6-8 in winter. Never allow moisture stress.

fertilizer.organic: 10 kg FYM per pit + neem cake 1 kg per plant

pests.name: Banana bunchy top virus
pests.prevention: Use virus-free planting material, control aphid vector

banana.harvestIndicators: Fingers become round (not angular), light green color, 75-80% maturity, 12-14 months from planting
banana.weatherSensitivity: Wind damage at fruiting. Frost kills plants. Needs consistently warm and humid conditions.

mango.waterRequirement: Minimal during winter; 50-60 liters/tree/week in summer
mango.irrigationMethod: Basin or drip irrigation for young trees
mango.irrigationSchedule: No irrigation during flowering (Jan-Feb). Resume after fruit set. Regular watering during fruit development (March-May).

fertilizer.organic: 20-25 kg FYM per tree per year

pests.name: Anthracnose
pests.prevention: Spray Carbendazim before and after flowering

pests.name: Fruit fly
pests.prevention: Methyl eugenol traps, harvest at mature green stage

mango.harvestIndicators: Fruit shoulder develops, color turns from dark to light green, specific gravity test (fruit sinks in water)
mango.weatherSensitivity: Rain during flowering causes major crop loss. Dry period Nov-Feb is critical for good flowering. Hailstorms damage fruit.

grapes.irrigationSchedule: Daily drip adjusted by growth stage. Reduce water 15 days before harvest for sugar concentration.

fertilizer.organic: 15-20 kg FYM per vine per year

grapes.harvestIndicators: TSS 18-22 Brix, berry color fully developed, berries detach easily from pedicel
grapes.weatherSensitivity: Rain during ripening causes berry cracking. Needs hot dry days for sugar accumulation. High humidity causes mildew.

apple.irrigationMethod: Drip irrigation preferred in modern orchards
apple.irrigationSchedule: Regular irrigation April-September. Critical during fruit development (May-July). Reduce before harvest.

pests.name: Apple scab
pests.prevention: Spray Mancozeb at green tip to petal fall

apple.harvestIndicators: Seed color turns brown, flesh is crisp, starch-iodine test, ground color changes from green to yellow
apple.weatherSensitivity: Needs 1000-1500 chilling hours. Hailstorms damage fruit. Unseasonal warm winters reduce fruit set.

orange.irrigationSchedule: Withhold water in Jan-Feb to induce flowering. Resume at fruit set. Regular watering during fruit development.

fertilizer.organic: 20 kg FYM per tree per year

orange.harvestIndicators: Fruit turns orange, juice content > 40%, TSS:acid ratio 10-12:1
orange.weatherSensitivity: Drought stress needed to trigger flowering. Excess rain during flowering causes fruit drop. Hot dry winds cause fruit drop.

papaya.irrigationSchedule: Regular watering every 3-4 days. Never allow waterlogging. Reduce in winter.

pests.name: Papaya ring spot virus
pests.prevention: Use tolerant varieties, remove infected plants immediately

pests.name: Mealybug
pests.prevention: Spray neem oil, release ladybird beetles

pests.name: Stem rot
pests.prevention: Avoid waterlogging, drench with Copper oxychloride

papaya.harvestIndicators: Fruit shows yellow streaks, latex turns watery (not milky), slight softening at blossom end
papaya.weatherSensitivity: Frost kills plants. Cannot tolerate waterlogging. Needs warm weather year-round. Wind damage common.

coconut.irrigationSchedule: Weekly irrigation in summer (40-50 liters/palm). Monsoon season usually sufficient. Mulch basins.

fertilizer.organic: 25 kg FYM + 5 kg neem cake per palm

pests.name: Rhinoceros beetle
pests.prevention: Fill leaf axils with neem + sand mixture

pests.name: Red palm weevil
pests.prevention: Avoid trunk injuries, inject Monocrotophos into bore holes

pests.name: Bud rot
pests.prevention: Pour Bordeaux mixture into crown during monsoon

coconut.harvestIndicators: Nuts at 11-12 months age, husk turns brown, water sound when shaken, harvest every 45 days
coconut.weatherSensitivity: Needs consistent warmth and moisture. Long droughts reduce nut size. Cyclonic winds break fronds and cause nut fall.

coffee.irrigationMethod: Sprinkler or drip under shade trees
coffee.irrigationSchedule: Blossom showers trigger flowering (March-April). Backing showers set fruit. Regular irrigation during fruit development.

pests.name: Coffee berry borer
pests.prevention: Spray Beauveria bassiana, strip-pick ripe berries

pests.name: White stem borer
pests.prevention: Swab trunks with Chlorpyriphos, remove affected branches

pests.name: Leaf rust (Hemileia)
pests.prevention: Spray Bordeaux mixture, use resistant varieties

coffee.harvestIndicators: Berries turn bright red (cherry stage), pick only ripe red cherries for quality
coffee.weatherSensitivity: Needs shade. Frost kills plants. Excess sun causes leaf scorch. Erratic rain disrupts flowering synchronization.

pomegranate.irrigationSchedule: Withhold water for 2 months to induce stress (bahar treatment). Resume at new flush. Regular drip during fruit development.

fertilizer.organic: 20 kg FYM + 2 kg neem cake per plant

pests.name: Bacterial blight (Oily spot)
pests.prevention: Spray Streptocycline + Copper oxychloride, prune affected parts

pomegranate.harvestIndicators: Skin turns red/pink, calyx closes, metallic sound when tapped, 5-6 months from flowering
pomegranate.weatherSensitivity: Heavy rain during fruiting causes cracking. Hailstorms damage fruit skin. Needs dry period for bahar regulation.

watermelon.irrigationSchedule: Regular watering every 4-5 days. Reduce 1 week before harvest to concentrate sugars. Avoid wetting foliage.

pests.name: Fruit fly
pests.prevention: Cue-lure traps, spray Malathion + jaggery bait

watermelon.harvestIndicators: Tendril nearest fruit dries, ground spot turns yellow, hollow sound when tapped, 75-90 days from sowing
watermelon.weatherSensitivity: Cannot tolerate frost. Needs hot sunny days for sweetness. Excess rain causes root rot and reduces sugar.

muskmelon.irrigationSchedule: Frequent light irrigation. Reduce water at fruit maturity for flavor. Avoid waterlogging.
muskmelon.harvestIndicators: Fruit develops aroma, changes color, slight softening at blossom end, netting fully developed, slip stage (detaches easily from vine)
muskmelon.weatherSensitivity: Needs hot dry weather for sweetness. Excess humidity causes powdery mildew. Cannot tolerate waterlogging.
